title: Skill: Seller Net Sheet Calculator
description:
## Overview
Calculate estimated seller proceeds from a home sale. Agent says "net sheet for 450k sale at 123 Main" and gets an itemized breakdown of costs and net proceeds instantly.

## Why This Matters
- Used at every listing appointment — sellers always ask "what will I walk away with?"
- Agents currently do napkin math or use title company websites
- No MLS tool provides this (RatePlug is buyer-focused mortgage calc)
- Instant credibility tool during client meetings
- Can compare multiple price scenarios side-by-side

## Skill Design: ~/.kiro/skills/net-sheet/SKILL.md

### Trigger Phrases
"net sheet", "seller net", "what does seller net", "seller proceeds", "what will I walk away with", "closing costs for selling"

### Inputs (from user)
Required:
- Sale price (or multiple prices for comparison)
Optional (with smart defaults):
- Listing agent commission % (default: 3%)
- Buyer agent commission % (default: 3%)
- Mortgage payoff amount (default: "unknown — excluded")
- Repair credits (default: $0)
- Home warranty (default: $0)
- Closing date (default: 30 days from today, for tax proration)
- Property address (to auto-pull tax data from MLS)

### Texas-Specific Line Items & Calculations

1. Sale Price (credit)
2. Listing Agent Commission: sale_price × listing_rate
3. Buyer Agent Commission: sale_price × buyer_rate
4. Owner's Title Insurance (TX state-regulated rates):
   - $0-$100K: $5.75 per $1,000
   - $100K-$1M: $5.00 per $1,000 on amount over $100K
   - $1M-$5M: $2.50 per $1,000 on amount over $1M
   - $5M-$15M: $2.25 per $1,000 on amount over $5M
   - $15M+: $2.00 per $1,000 on amount over $15M
   (Note: TX title insurance is state-regulated — same rates everywhere)
5. Title/Escrow/Settlement Fees: ~$1,500-$2,000 (default $1,800)
6. Recording Fees: ~$25-$75 (default $50)
7. Property Tax Proration:
   - If address provided: pull TaxAssessedValue from MLS, use county rate
   - County rates: Travis ~1.8%, Williamson ~2.1%, Hays ~1.9%, Bastrop ~1.9%, Caldwell ~1.8%
   - Formula: (assessed_value × rate) ÷ 365 × days_owned_this_year
   - If no address: estimate at 1.9% of sale price ÷ 365 × days
8. Transfer Tax: $0 (Texas has NO transfer tax)
9. Mortgage Payoff: user-provided or excluded
10. Repair Credits: user-provided
11. Home Warranty: user-provided (default $0, typical $450-$600)
12. HOA Transfer/Estoppel Fee: if applicable, ~$150-$300

= Estimated Net Proceeds

### Output Format
Show clean itemized table in chat:
```
Seller Net Sheet — 123 Main St, Austin TX
Sale Price:                    $450,000
─────────────────────────────────────────
Listing Agent (3.0%):         -$13,500
Buyer Agent (3.0%):           -$13,500
Owner's Title Insurance:      -$2,325
Title/Escrow Fees:            -$1,800
Recording Fees:               -$50
Property Tax Proration:       -$2,430
Transfer Tax:                 $0 (TX)
Mortgage Payoff:              -$225,000
─────────────────────────────────────────
ESTIMATED NET PROCEEDS:       $191,395
```

### Multi-Scenario Comparison
If agent provides multiple prices ("net sheet for 425k, 450k, 475k"):
Show side-by-side comparison table with all three scenarios.

### Optional PDF Output
If agent says "PDF net sheet" or "print net sheet", generate a clean one-page PDF using the pdf-report skill. Include:
- Agent/brokerage branding placeholder
- Date prepared
- Disclaimer: "This is an estimate only. Actual costs may vary."

### Implementation
Prompt-only skill — pure math, no external APIs. Optionally uses MLS skill to pull TaxAssessedValue and county for tax proration.

## Build Estimate
~30 minutes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Skill file created at ~/.kiro/skills/net-sheet/SKILL.md
- [ ] Calculates correct TX title insurance using state-regulated tiered rates
- [ ] Property tax proration works with and without MLS data
- [ ] Multi-scenario comparison works
- [ ] All line items match what a TX title company would produce
- [ ] Disclaimer included
- [ ] Optional PDF output works via pdf-report skill
close_reason: Skill file created at ~/.kiro/skills/net-sheet/SKILL.md. Lean design — only TX-specific data the model can't infer (regulated title rates, county tax rates, TX conventions). 54 lines, 2.1KB.

title: AI Listing Presentation Prep — Research & Plan
close_reason: Research complete. Plan at docs/listing-presentation-prep-plan.md. Discord command (!prep <address>) triggers Trestle API queries for subject property, closed comps (6mo), active competition, pending count. Calculates price/sqft, absorption rate, months of supply, DOM/price trends. Generates suggested price range + AI talking points. PDF via S3 presigned URL optional. Shares Trestle client and comp engine with CMA epic (bd-2mb). Blocked on Trestle credentials (bd-2mb.7).

title: Market Pulse Digest — Daily MLS Intelligence Bot
description: Research complete. Daily/weekly MLS digest delivered to Discord. Queries Trestle API for changes in target North Texas zip codes (new listings, price reductions, pending, sold, expired, back on market). Adds AI insights per listing. Weekly summary with trends, absorption rate, inventory. Shares Trestle client infrastructure with CMA and Lead bot. Full plan at docs/market-pulse-digest-plan.md. Requires IDX Plus feed minimum for sold data.
close_reason: Broker already receives daily market pulse info — no need to build this

title: AI CMA Generator - Trestle MLS Integration
close_reason: Replaced by CMA skill — all phases consolidated into skill-based approach

title: Trestle API Research & CMA Build Plan
description: Research complete. Deep review on 2026-03-10 identified 11 gaps in original plan (Section 12 of docs/cma-generator-plan.md): (1) Rate limiting must be baked into API client — token bucket, dynamic header reading, 429 backoff, per-CMA call budgeting. (2) Missing active listings + pending sales analysis. (3) Texas is non-disclosure state — MLS is only source of sold data. (4) Seller concessions distort ClosePrice. (5) Distressed sales need filtering/flagging. (6) Adjustment values should be derived from data, not hardcoded. (7) DOM context for comp weighting. (8) Subject property may not be in MLS — need manual input fallback. (9) Comp ranking needs subdivision weight increase, subtype matching, age bands. (10) Report needs confidence indicators. (11) Updated API call budget: 4-6 calls per CMA. Bridge API research also added (Section 11).
close_reason: Research complete. Plan documented with 12 sections covering API details, gaps, and corrections.

title: Phase 1: Trestle API Client (auth, query builder, media)
close_reason: Trestle API client complete in mls/ with 66 tests — absorbed into MLS skill

title: Phase 2: Comp Selection Engine (search, rank, score)
close_reason: Replaced by CMA skill approach — comp selection handled within the CMA skill instead of a standalone phase

title: Phase 3: Price Analysis (adjustments, range, market stats)
close_reason: Price analysis logic will live in CMA skill valuation step

title: Phase 4: Report Generation (narrative, PDF, map)
close_reason: Report generation handled by pdf-report skill, orchestrated by CMA skill

title: Phase 5: Agent Interface (conversational, refinement)
close_reason: Agent interface is the CMA skill itself — conversational by design

title: BLOCKER: Waiting on Trestle credentials + verify feed type is IDX Plus or higher
description: Trestle auth works but no active MLO connection. NTREIS IDX Plus available (00 setup + 6/mo + 00/mo platform). Emailed broker to ask about direct NTREIS credentials. Meanwhile, building against free Bridge Interactive ACTRIS reference server (Austin data, same RESO schema). Bridge access pending approval.

title: Skill: Contract Deadline Tracker
description:
## Overview
Track critical contract deadlines for active real estate transactions and send proactive reminders. Agent says "under contract 123 Main, 7 day option, closing April 15" and the bot tracks all dates and reminds them before each deadline.

## Why This Matters
- Missing a contract deadline can cost thousands (lost earnest money, default, blown deal)
- Agents juggle 5-10 active transactions simultaneously
- TransactionDesk (MLS tool) manages documents/signatures but does NOT send deadline reminders
- This is the most "AI agent working in the background" skill — proactive, not reactive
- Differentiator: no MLS tool or common agent tool does this well

## Skill Design: ~/.kiro/skills/deadline-tracker/SKILL.md

### Trigger Phrases
"under contract", "went under contract", "track deadlines for", "contract deadlines", "option ends", "closing date", "deadline check", "what's coming up"

### Architecture Decision: Beads for Persistence

Use `br` (beads-rust) as the persistence layer instead of a custom JSON file.

**Why beads over JSON:**
- `br` IS a task tracker with due dates — contract deadlines are literally tasks with due dates
- Git-native: transactions are version controlled automatically via `.beads/` JSONL sync
- Already has: statuses (open/in_progress/closed), due dates, labels, parent-child relationships, descriptions
- No new infrastructure needed — `br` commands are fast and atomic
- Transaction history preserved in git forever
- Can use `br list --label deadline` to query upcoming deadlines

**Why NOT JSON:**
- Would need custom read/write logic
- No git sync built in
- Reinventing what `br` already does

### Data Model (Beads)

**Transaction = Parent Bead (epic)**
```
Title: "TX: 123 Main St, Austin TX 78734"
Type: epic
Labels: transaction, listing-side (or buyer-side)
Description: "Effective: 2026-03-10 | Closing: 2026-04-15 | Notes: ..."
Status: open (active) -> closed (completed/fell through)
```

**Deadline = Child Bead (task, under parent)**
```
Title: "Option Period Ends - 123 Main St"
Type: task
Labels: deadline, option-period
Due: 2026-03-17
Parent: [transaction bead ID]
Status: open (pending) -> closed (complete/waived)
```

**Label taxonomy for deadlines:**
- deadline,earnest-money
- deadline,option-period
- deadline,title-commitment
- deadline,survey
- deadline,inspection
- deadline,financing
- deadline,appraisal
- deadline,closing-disclosure
- deadline,walkthrough
- deadline,closing

### Example br Commands the Skill Uses

```bash
# Create transaction
br create "TX: 123 Main St, Austin TX 78734" -t epic -p 0 -l transaction,listing-side \
  -d "Effective: 2026-03-10 | Closing: 2026-04-15"

# Create deadline under transaction (using --parent)
br create "Option Period Ends - 123 Main St" -t task -p 0 \
  -l deadline,option-period --parent bd-XXX --due 2026-03-17

br create "Earnest Money Due - 123 Main St" -t task -p 0 \
  -l deadline,earnest-money --parent bd-XXX --due 2026-03-13

br create "Title Commitment Due - 123 Main St" -t task -p 1 \
  -l deadline,title-commitment --parent bd-XXX --due 2026-03-30

br create "Financing Approval - 123 Main St" -t task -p 1 \
  -l deadline,financing --parent bd-XXX --due 2026-04-05

br create "Closing - 123 Main St" -t task -p 0 \
  -l deadline,closing --parent bd-XXX --due 2026-04-15

# Check upcoming deadlines
br list --label deadline

# Mark deadline complete
br close bd-YYY --reason "Option period complete, proceeding"

# Close entire transaction
br close bd-XXX --reason "Closed successfully on 2026-04-15"
```

### Texas TREC Contract Deadlines (Standard)

| Deadline | Default Timing | TREC Para | Label |
|----------|---------------|-----------|-------|
| Earnest money deposit | 3 days after effective | Para 5 | deadline,earnest-money |
| Option period ends | 5-10 days (negotiated) | Para 23 | deadline,option-period |
| Title commitment delivery | 20 days after effective | Para 6.A | deadline,title-commitment |
| Survey delivery | per contract terms | Para 6.C | deadline,survey |
| Property inspection | during option period | Para 10 | deadline,inspection |
| Financing approval | 21-30 days (negotiated) | Para 4 | deadline,financing |
| Appraisal | 10-14 days (lender-driven) | N/A | deadline,appraisal |
| Closing Disclosure | 3 biz days before closing | Federal | deadline,closing-disclosure |
| Final walkthrough | 1-2 days before closing | Para 7.E | deadline,walkthrough |
| Closing date | 30-45 days (negotiated) | Para 9 | deadline,closing |

### Input Parsing
Agent can provide dates in natural language:
- "under contract 123 Main, effective March 10, 7 day option, closing April 15"
- "just went UC at 456 Oak, option 10 days, closing 35 days out"
- "track: 789 Elm, effective today, option ends March 18, financing March 30, closing April 20"

Skill should:
1. Parse the effective date (or default to today)
2. Calculate all deadlines from effective date using provided or default timings
3. Let agent override any specific date
4. Create parent bead (transaction) + child beads (each deadline) via br commands
5. Confirm all dates back to the agent for verification

### Reminder System

**Option A: On-demand (build first)**
- Agent asks "what's coming up?" or "deadline check"
- Skill runs: `br list --label deadline` and filters by due date proximity
- Reports deadlines within 48 hours with warning emoji
- Also check at session startup if any deadlines are within 48 hours

**Option B: Scheduled (enhancement, later)**
- Cron job runs `br list --label deadline` and filters for next 48 hours
- Posts to Discord via webhook if any results
- 20-line Python script + cron entry

Start with Option A.

### Commands
- "under contract [address], [details]" -> create transaction bead + deadline child beads
- "deadline check" / "what's coming up?" -> `br list --label deadline`, show upcoming
- "mark [deadline] complete for [address]" -> `br close` the deadline bead
- "push closing to [date] for [address]" -> `br update --due [date]` the deadline bead
- "closed [address]" / "deal fell through [address]" -> `br close` transaction + all children
- "show all transactions" -> `br list --label transaction`

### Output Format
```
Active Transactions (3)

  123 Main St, Austin TX 78734 (bd-XXX)
   Effective: March 10 | Closing: April 15
   [!] Option Period Ends: March 17 (in 2 days)
   [ ] Title Commitment Due: March 30
   [ ] Financing Approval: April 5
   [ ] Closing: April 15

  456 Oak Dr, Round Rock TX 78681 (bd-YYY)
   Effective: March 5 | Closing: April 10
   [x] Earnest Money: Deposited
   [x] Option Period: Complete
   [ ] Financing Approval: April 1 (in 5 days)
```

## Build Estimate
~1 hour for Option A (skill file + test with sample transactions)
~30 min additional for Option B (cron script + webhook)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Skill file created at ~/.kiro/skills/deadline-tracker/SKILL.md
- [ ] Can create a new transaction from natural language input via br create
- [ ] Calculates all TREC deadlines from effective date
- [ ] Each deadline is a child bead with due date and labels
- [ ] "deadline check" shows all upcoming deadlines with status indicators
- [ ] Can mark deadlines complete via br close
- [ ] Can modify dates (br update --due), archive transactions
- [ ] Warns about deadlines within 48 hours
- [ ] Handles multiple simultaneous transactions
- [ ] Tested with realistic scenarios (listing side, buyer side)
close_reason: Skill file created at ~/.kiro/skills/deadline-tracker/SKILL.md. Beads-backed persistence — transactions as epics, deadlines as child tasks with due dates and labels. 81 lines, 3.4KB.

title: Skill: Listing Description Writer
description:
## Overview
Generate MLS remarks, social media captions, and email copy from MLS property data. Agent says "write listing for 123 Main St" and gets publish-ready content in seconds.

## Why This Matters
- Agents spend 20-45 min per listing writing descriptions manually
- No MLS tool does this (AdBuilder makes ads, not remarks)
- Existing paid tools ($10-20/listing) require manual data entry — we auto-pull from MLS
- Used at every single new listing — high frequency

## Skill Design: ~/.kiro/skills/listing-writer/SKILL.md

### Trigger Phrases
"write listing for", "listing remarks", "MLS description for", "social post for", "write remarks"

### Workflow
1. Look up property in MLS by address or ListingKey
2. Collect: address, beds, baths, sqft, year built, lot size, subdivision, property subtype, garage, pool, interior/exterior features, construction materials, roof, heating, cooling, view, flooring, public remarks
3. Generate three outputs:
   - MLS Remarks (250-1000 chars): Professional, factual, highlights best features. Lead with strongest selling point. End with call to action.
   - Social Caption (Instagram/Facebook): Casual tone, emoji, 3-5 hashtags, under 300 chars
   - Email Blast Copy: 2-3 paragraphs, narrative style, paints a picture of living there
4. Optional tone parameter: luxury, investor, first-time-buyer, downsizer (default: general)

### Fair Housing Compliance (CRITICAL)
Skill MUST enforce Fair Housing Act compliance automatically:
- NEVER reference protected classes: race, color, religion, national origin, sex, familial status, disability
- BANNED phrases: "family-friendly", "perfect for families", "great for couples", "bachelor pad", "man cave", "walking distance to [church/synagogue/mosque]", "exclusive neighborhood", "safe neighborhood", "no children", "adults only" (unless 55+ community)
- Use "primary bedroom/suite" NOT "master bedroom/suite"
- Describe the PROPERTY and its FEATURES only — never describe who should live there
- Include compliance note: "All descriptions comply with Fair Housing Act. Describes property features only."

### MLS Fields to Pull
- Identity: UnparsedAddress, City, PostalCode, SubdivisionName
- Structure: BedroomsTotal, BathroomsTotalInteger, BathroomsFull, BathroomsHalf, LivingArea, LotSizeSquareFeet, LotSizeAcres, YearBuilt, StoriesTotal, GarageSpaces, PoolPrivateYN
- Features: PropertyType, PropertySubType, ConstructionMaterials, Roof, Heating, Cooling, View, Flooring, InteriorFeatures, ExteriorFeatures
- Description: PublicRemarks (existing, for reference/rewrite)
- Media: Media array (for context)

### Output Format
Show all three outputs clearly labeled in chat. Agent can copy-paste directly. No PDF needed.

### Implementation
Prompt-only skill — no Python, no external APIs. Just MLS data pull via mls skill + LLM reasoning.

## Build Estimate
~30 minutes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Skill file created at ~/.kiro/skills/listing-writer/SKILL.md
- [ ] Generates MLS remarks, social caption, email copy from any MLS address
- [ ] Fair Housing compliance enforced (test with edge cases)
- [ ] Works with Bridge actris_ref data
- [ ] Tone parameter works (luxury, investor, etc.)
- [ ] Tested on 3+ property types (SFR, condo, townhouse)
close_reason: Skill file created at ~/.kiro/skills/listing-writer/SKILL.md. Generates MLS remarks, social captions, email copy with Fair Housing compliance. Tested against Bridge actris_ref data.

title: AI Lead Qualification & Routing Bot
description: Research complete. Discord-first approach — broker interacts via Discord channel. Paste address → property analysis. Paste lead → scoring. Request full CMA → PDF generated via weasyprint, uploaded to S3, presigned URL posted in Discord (7-day expiry). Trestle API for property data. Full plan at docs/lead-qualification-bot-plan.md.
